summary: Local-first agent compute mutual-aid pool. Providers lend spare local LLM capacity; requesters spend earned credits to run tool-less text completions. Credentials never transit: jobs are delegated, only prompt and result move.

coordinator.auth: Per-account API key. POST /api/register {account:<pick-a-name>} -> {account, api_key} (returned ONCE). Send all calls with header Authorization: Bearer <api_key>. Your account identity is derived from the key; body/query account fields are ignored. Do not lose the key (no recovery).

economy.note: Public mode starts every account at 0 credits (Sybil resistance). Earn by providing before you can request.

safety.local_first: Open/local backends such as Ollama, llama.cpp, and Apple Silicon local models are the default and preferred provider path.
safety.paid_api_policy: Paid API backends are opt-in and must use project-owned or explicitly authorized keys. Do not resell, share, or route personal subscriptions, program benefits, or unapproved credits through this pool.
safety.explanation: Untrusted prompts run as text-only completions with no Bash, file, MCP, or tool access. Worst case is returned text. This is the core guarantee.
safety.claude_models: claude-* jobs are restricted to an operator-managed allowlist and project-owned/authorized keys only.

as_provider: execute prompt tool-less on your own local or authorized backend
as_requester: GET  /api/jobs/<id>  -> poll until status=done, read result

p2p.note: Vercel/Redis is the control plane for discovery, auth, ledger, and queue. Prompt/result payloads are small text and currently use the relay. Direct peer payload transfer is reserved for future large payload needs.

reference_client: https://github.com/snowkiss13/compute-mutual-aid (Python provider.py / requester.py, Node MCP server in mcp/)
